sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
Clustered collection limitations:

- The clustered index key must be on the `_id` field.

- Clustered collections may not be [capped collections](manual/core/capped-collections).

sections.heading: Step 1: Open the  dialog box.
sections.body:
Select a database and from the **Collections** screen, click the
**Create Collection** button.

You can also click the `+` next to the name of the database you select
to open the **Create Collection** dialog box.

sections.heading: Step 3: Select the type of collection you want to create.
sections.body:
From the **Additional preferences** drop-down, select
**Clustered Collections**.

sections.heading: Step 4: (Optional) Name your clustered index.
sections.body:
You can enter a name for the clustered index or use the automatically
generated name.

sections.heading: Step 5: (Optional) Enter the number of seconds for the  field.
sections.body:
The **expireAfterSeconds** field is a [TTL index](manual/tutorial/expire-data)
that enables automatic deletion of documents older than the specified
number of seconds. The **expireAfterSeconds** field must be a positive,
non-zero value.

sections.heading: Step 6: Click  to create your new collection.
sections.body:
In the **Collections** screen, your new collection is marked by a
**Clustered** badge next to the collection name.
